description: Evaluates whether generated code faithfully implements the requirements defined in a spec.

criteria.name: requirement_coverage
criteria.description: All requirements and acceptance criteria from the spec are implemented.

scoring.5: Every requirement and acceptance criterion is implemented. No gaps.
scoring.4: All major requirements implemented. Minor acceptance criteria partially addressed.
scoring.3: Most requirements implemented. Some acceptance criteria missing.
scoring.2: Significant requirements missing. Only happy path covered.
scoring.1: Most requirements unimplemented or fundamentally misunderstood.

criteria.name: interface_adherence
criteria.description: Code matches the interfaces, signatures, and contracts defined in the spec.

scoring.5: All function signatures, API contracts, and data structures exactly match the spec.
scoring.4: Interfaces match with minor naming or type differences that don't break contracts.
scoring.3: Most interfaces match. Some deviations from spec'd contracts.
scoring.1: Interfaces bear little resemblance to the spec.

criteria.name: edge_case_handling
criteria.description: Edge cases and error scenarios described in the spec are handled.

scoring.5: All edge cases and error scenarios from the spec are handled gracefully.
scoring.4: Most edge cases handled. Minor spec-defined scenarios missed.
scoring.3: Common edge cases handled. Some spec-defined error paths missing.
scoring.2: Only happy path. Most spec-defined edge cases unhandled.
scoring.1: No edge case handling despite spec requirements.

criteria.name: spec_intent_fidelity
criteria.description: Implementation captures the intent and spirit of the spec, not just the letter.

scoring.5: Implementation fully captures the spec's intent, including implied behaviors and user expectations.
scoring.4: Captures intent well. Minor misalignment with spec's broader goals.
scoring.3: Technically meets requirements but misses some of the spec's intent.
scoring.2: Follows spec literally but misses the underlying purpose.

output_format.overall_score: float 0-1 (weighted average of criteria scores normalized to 0-1)
output_format.criteria_scores: object mapping criteria name to { score: 1-5, reasoning: string }
output_format.spec_gaps: array of strings describing unimplemented requirements
